name: Half-typed message survives a page reload via localStorage
description: AsstComposer (and the AsstChat composer) writes the in-flight draft to localStorage on every keystroke (key: 'pm:composer:{conversationId|new}'). On mount, it reads back. Reload, route navigation, or accidental tab close shouldn't lose the user's typing.

persona.name: Diego
persona.context: Phone rang — by the time he hung up, the page had reloaded due to a deploy.

steps.action: Types 'Quote a fence repair for the Patels — labor $350 plus materi' (incomplete) but does NOT hit send

steps.expect: Textarea remounts pre-populated with the same partial text; cursor focused at end
Message sends as expected; localStorage entry is removed (cleanup on successful send)

steps.action: Starts typing again, then closes the browser tab without sending
steps.expect: Re-opening /assistant later restores the unsent draft from localStorage

evaluationCriteria.check: Restored text appears immediately on mount — no flash of empty textarea
User feels safe typing long messages; no surprise data loss on accidental refresh
Storage key includes conversationId so drafts don't bleed across threads; key for /assistant (no thread) uses 'new' suffix
Restoring text doesn't move the screen-reader focus; the textarea's aria-label is unchanged
